title: Playlist with Composition and Initializer List
prompt_md:
## Goal

Build a `Playlist` that **composes** `Song` objects and accepts an initial set via `std::initializer_list`, with the ability to add more songs afterward.

## Input

- Lines 1-3: three songs for the initial playlist, each a title (single word) and an integer duration in seconds.
- Line 4: integer `N` (0 <= N <= 10), the number of songs to add after construction.
- Next `N` lines: a title (single word) and an integer duration in seconds.

## Output

The playlist printed twice, separated by a blank line: first the initial three songs, then the playlist after the `N` additions. Each printing is:

```
Playlist:
  <title> (<duration>s)
  ...
```

## Notes

- `Song` stores a title and duration and can print itself as `  <title> (<duration>s)`.
- `Playlist` owns its songs **by value** (`std::vector<Song>`), takes a `std::initializer_list<Song>` constructor, and supports adding a song.
- Read the three initial songs first, then construct the playlist with `Playlist{ Song{t1, d1}, Song{t2, d2}, Song{t3, d3} }` from those values. The initial contents come from input, so a hardcoded list of songs will not pass.

test_cases.label: Sample -- add two songs to initial three
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Playlist:
  Alpha (180s)
  Beta (210s)
  Gamma (95s)

Playlist:
  Alpha (180s)
  Beta (210s)
  Gamma (95s)
  Delta (150s)
  Epsilon (300s)

test_cases.label: Hidden -- different initial songs, add none
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Playlist:
  Sun (100s)
  Moon (200s)
  Star (50s)

Playlist:
  Sun (100s)
  Moon (200s)
  Star (50s)

test_cases.label: Hidden -- different initial songs, add one
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Playlist:
  Red (12s)
  Green (34s)
  Blue (56s)

Playlist:
  Red (12s)
  Green (34s)
  Blue (56s)
  Omega (42s)

test_cases.label: Hidden -- different initial songs, add three
test_cases.stdin:
One 10
Two 20
Three 30
3
Nova 60
Pulse 120
Echo 240
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Playlist:
  One (10s)
  Two (20s)
  Three (30s)

Playlist:
  One (10s)
  Two (20s)
  Three (30s)
  Nova (60s)
  Pulse (120s)
  Echo (240s)

title: School Registry: Container, Aggregation, and Dependencies
prompt_md:
## Goal

Model a school registry that combines three relationships: `Student` is an independent entity, `Classroom` **aggregates** students by non-owning pointer, and a free function `printRoster` **depends on** a classroom temporarily.

## Input

- Line 1: integer `S` (1 <= S <= 10), the number of students.
- Next `S` lines: a student name (single word) and an integer grade (0-100).
- Next line: integer `C` (1 <= C <= 3), the number of classrooms.
- Then `C` classroom blocks: a line with the classroom name (single word) and a count `K`, followed by a line of `K` space-separated 0-based student indices.

## Output

For each classroom, in input order:

```
<name> roster:
  <student> (grade: <grade>)
  ...
Average: <avg>
```

where `<avg>` is the **truncated integer average** of that classroom's grades. Separate consecutive classrooms with a blank line.

## Notes

- Students live in one place (e.g. a `std::vector<Student>`); classrooms hold only pointers to them and never own them.
- `Classroom` can compute its own average grade; `printRoster` is a free function taking a `const Classroom&` that prints the block above.

test_cases.label: Sample -- two classrooms with shared students
test_cases.stdin:
4
Alice 90
Bob 75
Clara 88
Dave 62
2
Math 3
0 1 3
Science 2
2 3
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Math roster:
  Alice (grade: 90)
  Bob (grade: 75)
  Dave (grade: 62)
Average: 75

Science roster:
  Clara (grade: 88)
  Dave (grade: 62)
Average: 75

test_cases.label: Hidden -- single classroom with one student
Hidden -- three classrooms with truncated averages
test_cases.stdin:
3
Frank 81
Grace 67
Hank 44
3
History 2
0 2
English 3
0 1 2
Gym 1
1
test_cases.expected_stdout:
History roster:
  Frank (grade: 81)
  Hank (grade: 44)
Average: 62

English roster:
  Frank (grade: 81)
  Grace (grade: 67)
  Hank (grade: 44)
Average: 64

Gym roster:
  Grace (grade: 67)
Average: 67
